demographics.living_situation: lives at home with spouse, who is present and made the call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Please help — I suddenly can't catch my breath, especially when I lie down, my chest feels like it's drowning, and my head is pounding so hard I can barely think. My spouse says my lips look blue.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on suddenly this evening and it's getting worse fast — I have to sit bolt upright just to breathe.

symptom_narrative.onset: came on suddenly this evening, out of nowhere
symptom_narrative.duration: the last hour or so and getting worse
symptom_narrative.character: can't catch my breath, chest feels wet and heavy like it's drowning, a bit of frothy spit when I cough, and a relentless pounding headache
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst I've ever felt — I can't finish a sentence without gasping
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and worsening; much worse when I try to lie back
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting bolt upright, leaning forward over the table
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lying down or leaning back makes it feel like I'm suffocating
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: sweaty and clammy, feel cold, pounding headache, spouse says I look pale and my lips are blue
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this before, but my blood pressure has been terrible to control for the last month
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: can't move from the chair, can't lie down, can't speak in full sentences

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: very high blood pressure that has been really hard to control this past month
past_medical_history.how_managed: on three different blood pressure tablets including a fluid tablet

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: hardening of the arteries and heart artery disease
my kidney blood tests got worse recently, right after my doctor started me on a new blood pressure tablet

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a new blood pressure tablet my regular doctor started a few weeks ago (perindopril)
two other blood pressure tablets, one of them a fluid/water tablet

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: older adult, lives with spouse; spouse is present and in a panic

objective_data_offered.value: 225/120 on our home blood pressure machine just now
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on an uncalibrated home cuff during acute distress

objective_data_offered.value: bluish, dusky lips; pale, sweaty face; sitting bolt upright and leaning forward over a table, using neck and shoulder muscles to breathe, unable to finish a sentence
spouse reports he is gasping, coughing up frothy spit, cold and clammy to touch, and looks like he is struggling badly to breathe
